root_cause.text: The `MultiJobResumeBuild` action did not perform authorization checks before execution, allowing any user with basic read access to trigger a build resumption.
root_cause.evidence: The patch implements the `StaplerProxy` interface and adds a `getTarget()` method that explicitly calls `Jenkins.getInstance().checkPermission(Jenkins.ADMINISTER)` to enforce proper access control before returning the action object.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The `MultiJobResumeBuild` action did not perform authorization checks before execution, allowing any user with basic read access to trigger a build resumption.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch implements the `StaplerProxy` interface and adds a `getTarget()` method that explicitly calls `Jenkins.getInstance().checkPermission(Jenkins.ADMINISTER)` to enforce proper access control before returning the action object.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add import for StaplerProxy to support upcoming class changes.
intent_record.what: Adds an import statement for org.kohsuke.stapler.StaplerProxy.
intent_record.how: Makes the StaplerProxy class available for use within the current Java file.

intent_record.intent_summary: Implement StaplerProxy to support web request delegation.
intent_record.what: Added StaplerProxy to the implements list.
intent_record.how: Integrates with Stapler framework for request delegation.

intent_record.intent_summary: Restrict visibility of the resume build icon to users with administrator permissions.
intent_record.what: Modified the return statement in getIconFileName to use a ternary operator that checks for Jenkins.ADMINISTER permissions.
intent_record.how: Hides the resume build icon from the UI by returning null if the current user does not have administrative privileges.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add permission check to MultiJobResumeBuild to restrict access to administrators.
intent_record.what: Added a getTarget method that checks for ADMINISTER permission before returning the object.
intent_record.how: Restricts access to the action by requiring administrative privileges, preventing unauthorized access.

grouping_result.intent: Add import for StaplerProxy to support upcoming class changes.
Implement StaplerProxy to support web request delegation.
Add permission check to MultiJobResumeBuild to restrict access to administrators.

consistency_review.core_intent: Restrict visibility and access of the resume build feature to administrators.

relevance_review.reason: The addition of the StaplerProxy import is a necessary structural change to implement the missing authorization checks required to fix the vulnerability.
Implementing StaplerProxy is a standard mechanism in Jenkins to delegate web requests and their associated permission checks to a parent object, directly addressing the missing authorization checks.
The group directly addresses the vulnerability by adding the missing authorization checks to the MultiJobResumeBuild action and restricting its visibility to administrators.

candidates.rationale: The class definition lacks implementation of an interface (like StaplerProxy) or permission checks to restrict execution to authorized users.
The method displaying the icon does not check permissions, making the action visible to unauthorized users.

vuln_statements.explanation: The class definition lacks implementation of StaplerProxy or permission checks to restrict execution to authorized users.
The method displaying the icon does not check permissions, making the action visible to unauthorized users.

Author: Sergey Shelomentsev <sergey.shelomentsev@xored.com>

    Fixes

diff --git a/src/main/java/com/tikal/jenkins/plugins/multijob/MultiJobResumeBuild.java b/src/main/java/com/tikal/jenkins/plugins/multijob/MultiJobResumeBuild.java
index 8da4e86..403628b 100644
--- a/src/main/java/com/tikal/jenkins/plugins/multijob/MultiJobResumeBuild.java
+++ b/src/main/java/com/tikal/jenkins/plugins/multijob/MultiJobResume
bic_trace_step.reason: The doIndex method in MultiJobResumeBuild schedules a build using project.scheduleBuild(0, cause, control) but does not perform any authorization checks (such as project.checkPermission(Item.BUILD)) beforehand, which leaves it vulnerable to unauthorized execution.
